title: [T2] Bootstrap: Python 3.12 venv + install vendored Evennia + evennia --init gridwars
description: Singleton bootstrap that creates the runnable Evennia game directory. Steps: (1) `python3.12 -m venv .venv` (NOT default python3 — that is 3.11, incompatible per T1 + S1 notes). (2) `.venv/bin/pip install -U pip setuptools wheel`. (3) Install vendored Evennia per T1 findings (likely `pip install -e ./vendor/evennia`). (4) Verify `.venv/bin/evennia --version` returns 6.0.0. (5) `cd` to repo root, run `.venv/bin/evennia --init gridwars` to generate the game directory. (6) Verify standard Evennia layout exists: commands/, typeclasses/, world/, web/, server/conf/settings.py. (7) Commit ONLY the generated gridwars/ tree (NOT the .venv — that is gitignored). Acceptance: `tree gridwars -L 2` shows the standard layout; venv is gitignored; commit message references T1 + Epic 2.
close_reason: bootstrap shipped via PR #7; gridwars/ standard layout generated; venv gitignored correctly

title: [T1] Research: Evennia init patterns + vendored install workflow
description: Look up best practice for using a VENDORED Evennia (vendor/evennia at v6.0.0) with `evennia --init`. Specifically resolve: (a) Should the venv use `pip install -e ./vendor/evennia` (editable from the submodule) OR a normal `pip install evennia==6.0.0` from PyPI? (b) Does `evennia --init <name>` require Evennia to be importable on $PATH, or does it accept a path arg? (c) What goes in gridwars/server/conf/settings.py vs the Evennia defaults? (d) Any known Python 3.12 + Django 6.x + Evennia v6 conflicts to anticipate? Output findings into bd notes via `bd update <id> --notes`. Read-only — no code changes. Feeds T2 (bootstrap singleton).
notes:
EVENNIA INIT + VENDORED INSTALL RESEARCH — 2026-05-24

## Recommended bootstrap sequence (T2)

```bash
python3.12 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install --upgrade pip setuptools
pip install -e ./vendor/evennia
evennia --init gridwars   # creates ./gridwars/ at cwd
cd gridwars
evennia migrate
evennia start
```

## One-line conclusions

1. **Editable vs. pinned**: `pip install -e ./vendor/evennia` (editable). Pinned-from-PyPI would defeat the point of vendoring. The submodule's commit pin prevents accidental drift.

2. **`evennia --init <name>`**: requires Evennia importable (venv activated). Takes a NAME (not path). Generates `./<name>/` relative to cwd → run from repo root to land at `./gridwars/`.

3. **Settings.py first-run knobs**: SERVER_NAME (auto-templated from gamedir name → defaults to "gridwars"; T4 should override to "GridWars.run"), TELNET_PORTS=[4000], WEBSOCKET_CLIENT_PORT=4002, WEBSERVER_PORTS=[(4001, 4005)], AMP_PORT=4006, DEBUG. Defaults inherit from `settings_default.py`; rarely need tuning for first launch.

4. **Python 3.12 + Django 6 + Evennia v6.0.0**: no known incompatibilities. v6.0.0 explicitly requires Python ≥3.12 and Django ≥6.0.2,<6.1 and Twisted ≥24.11. CHANGELOG documents distutils fix for 3.12.

5. **Migrations**: use `evennia migrate` (Evennia wrapper around Django migrate; triggers initial_setup → creates #1 superuser + Limbo room). NOT `python manage.py migrate` directly.

## Top 3 gotchas

1. Venv activation is mandatory — `evennia` not found = inactive venv.
2. `evennia --init gridwars` creates `./gridwars/` RELATIVE to cwd. Plan accordingly (we want repo root as cwd).
3. Python 3.11 not supported; explicitly use `python3.12` (default `python3` on this machine is 3.11.15 alias).

## Sources

- `vendor/evennia/pyproject.toml` (requires-python, django, twisted pins)
- `vendor/evennia/CHANGELOG.md` (v6.0.0 entries: dropped 3.11, distutils fix)
- `vendor/evennia/evennia/server/evennia_launcher.py:1401-1456, 2068-2073` (init mechanics)
- `vendor/evennia/README.md:25` (canonical first-run commands)
- https://www.evennia.com/docs/latest/Setup/Installation.html
- https://www.evennia.com/docs/latest/Setup/Installation-Git.html
- https://www.evennia.com/docs/latest/Setup/Settings.html
- https://www.evennia.com/docs/latest/Concepts/Server-Lifecycle.html

Full research agent transcript: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jayse-Code-gridwars-run/be5f1b1f-9991-44d9-8196-ee3dc2df875d/tasks/a1ce37414cfb5086b.output
close_reason: research complete; findings in bd notes; recommended bootstrap is pip install -e ./vendor/evennia + evennia --init gridwars + evennia migrate

title: [S3] Replace LICENSE with AGPL-3.0-or-later
description: Replace the current MIT LICENSE at repo root with the standard AGPL-3.0-or-later license text. Preserve copyright holder (Jay German, 2026). Use the canonical text from https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.txt. Commit message must surface the deliberate license change (MIT → AGPL-3.0-or-later) so the git log carries the rationale. Acceptance: `head -1 LICENSE` returns "GNU AFFERO GENERAL PUBLIC LICENSE"; full text matches GNU canonical; copyright line names Jay German 2026; SPDX line `SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later` present at top.
close_reason: shipped via PR #2

title: [S2] Vendor Evennia as pinned git submodule at vendor/evennia
description:
# Story — Vendor Evennia as pinned git submodule

## Why
GridWars depends on Evennia for networking, sessions, webclient, DB, typeclasses, etc. We vendor it (not pip-install) so contributors get a deterministic, hackable copy with our build, and so any future bespoke patches are reviewable in our tree. Submodule (over subtree or vendoring-by-copy) is the choice: smallest disk, reproducible, easy to bump.

## Scope
- Add Evennia as a git submodule at `vendor/evennia`:
  ```bash
  git submodule add https://github.com/evennia/evennia.git vendor/evennia
  cd vendor/evennia && git checkout <pinned-tag-from-S1> && cd ../..
  git add .gitmodules vendor/evennia
  ```
- Pin to the tag identified in story S1 (gridwars_run-… — research task).
- Verify `vendor/evennia/LICENSE.txt` (Evennia BSD-3) is present and untouched.
- Confirm `git submodule status` shows the pinned commit SHA (prefixed with `+` or `-` cleanly, no `U`).
- Do NOT modify anything inside `vendor/evennia/` — game code goes elsewhere (gridwars/ in Epic 2).

## Acceptance
- `git submodule status` lists `vendor/evennia` at the pinned commit, no dirty marker.
- `cat vendor/evennia/LICENSE.txt` shows BSD-3-Clause license header.
- `.gitmodules` records the upstream URL + path.
- No files modified inside `vendor/evennia/`.
- Commit message records the pinned tag + commit SHA for auditability.

## Out of scope
- Installing Evennia into a venv (Epic 2).
- Creating the `gridwars/` game directory (Epic 2).
- Editing or patching Evennia source (deferred indefinitely).

## Suggested platform
Codex builder — git submodule mechanics + verification.
close_reason: vendored Evennia v6.0.0 via PR #1 (github.com/jsgerman-oss/gridwars.run/pull/1)

title: [S1] Research: identify latest Evennia stable release tag
description: Look up the latest stable Evennia release tag and commit SHA from github.com/evennia/evennia (releases page + tags). Verify Python 3.11/3.12 compatibility. Output the tag, commit SHA, release date, release notes URL, and any pinning gotchas into bd notes via `bd update <id> --notes-file <path>`. Read-only — no code changes. This feeds S2 (vendoring) and S4 (THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES). Acceptance: bd notes carry tag, commit SHA, release URL, Python-version compat statement, and at least one pinning gotcha (or "none observed").
notes:
EVENNIA RELEASE LOOKUP — 2026-05-24

## Recommended pin
- **Tag**: `v6.0.0`
- **Commit SHA**: `966301fc1f0318224f7c78c3702131126075429b`
- **Released**: 2026-02-15
- **Tag URL**: https://github.com/evennia/evennia/releases/tag/v6.0.0
- **License**: BSD-3-Clause (pyproject `license = { text = "BSD" }`, full text in upstream `LICENSE.txt`)

## Python compatibility (IMPORTANT — load-bearing for Epic 2)
- v6.0.0 **drops Python 3.11 support** (backwards-incompatible change). Required: **3.12, 3.13, 3.14**.
- Local env: `python3` defaults to 3.11.15 (Homebrew alias). `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.12` and `python3.13` are both installed.
- ACTION for Epic 2 (gridwars_run-th6): the venv must be created with `python3.12 -m venv .venv` (or 3.13), NOT the default `python3`.

## Fallback option
- `v5.0.1` (2025-07-02, sha `9024f804f92738f87916c8c35759e3dfd81f7e52`) still supports Python 3.11.
- Use only if v6.0.0 conflicts with something downstream we discover. Default to v6.0.0 for forward-looking pinning.

## Pinning gotchas
- Evennia tags releases but does NOT publish GitHub Releases entries. `gh api repos/evennia/evennia/releases/latest` returns 404. Use `gh api repos/evennia/evennia/tags` instead.
- v6.0.0 bumps Django to ≥6.0.2 and DRF to 3.16 — confirm system pip can resolve these (it should; Django 6.x is current).
- `XYZGrid` contrib bumped scipy 1.15→1.17 (only relevant if we use that contrib, which we don't yet).

## Source confirmation
- pyproject @ v6.0.0: `requires-python = ">=3.12"`
- CHANGELOG @ v6.0.0: "Feat (backwards incompatble): Drop Python 3.11 support (supported: Python 3.12, 3.13, 3.14 (req))."
close_reason: Researched: pin to Evennia v6.0.0 @ 966301f. Requires Python 3.12+ (drops 3.11). Full notes attached.

title: [EPIC] Evennia Game Bootstrap
description:
# Epic 2 — Evennia Game Bootstrap

## Why
Evennia projects live in a generated "game directory" (`evennia --init <name>`). That directory is where all our custom typeclasses, commands, world scripts, and settings go. Until it exists and the server boots cleanly with migrations applied, no gameplay work can land. This epic gets us to "server starts, telnet on 4000, webclient on 4001, fresh admin can log in".

## Scope
- Create `gridwars/` game directory via `evennia --init gridwars` against the vendored Evennia.
- Wire dependencies: `pyproject.toml` (preferred) or `requirements.txt` pinning Evennia to the vendored version.
- Add `Makefile` (or `scripts/`) helpers: `make install`, `make migrate`, `make run`, `make stop`, `make test`.
- Configure `gridwars/server/conf/settings.py` minimally (DB path, ports, server name = "GridWars.run", debug off for prod profile).
- Run initial Django migrations.
- Confirm `evennia start` boots, telnet on `localhost:4000`, webclient on `http://localhost:4001`.
- Document the run procedure inline in README (full README polish is Epic 10).

## Acceptance
- `gridwars/` exists with Evennia's standard layout (`commands/`, `typeclasses/`, `world/`, `web/`, `server/`).
- `make install && make migrate && make run` brings up the server end-to-end.
- A fresh user can connect via telnet AND webclient and see the default Evennia welcome (theming is Epic 3/8).
- No edits inside `vendor/evennia`.
- `make test` runs Evennia's test harness against `gridwars/` (may be empty initially, must not error).

## Model recommendation
- **Builder (Codex)** — Python/Django/Evennia CLI fluency, migration mechanics.
- **Tester (Codex)** — verify boot + connection.

## Notes
- Pin Evennia version to match vendored submodule commit (Epic 1).
- Use SQLite for dev; Postgres consideration deferred.
- Blocks: Epics 3–9.
close_reason: Epic 2 (Evennia Game Bootstrap) complete: gridwars/ generated, Makefile + pyproject.toml + settings.py wired and fixed, boot verified end-to-end PR #11

title: [EPIC] Foundation, Vendoring & Licensing
description:
# Epic 1 — Foundation, Vendoring & Licensing

## Why
GridWars.run is an open-source AGPL game built on top of Evennia (BSD-3). Before we can write a single command or room, the repo needs to be set up correctly: Evennia vendored at a pinned version, license aligned with project intent, attribution preserved, and `.gitignore` covering Python/Evennia/SQLite runtime debris. Getting this wrong later means painful retroactive license cleanup or a tangled vendor tree.

## Scope
- Vendor `evennia/evennia` under `vendor/evennia` as a git submodule (preferred) pinned to the latest stable release tag.
- Document upstream URL + exact commit/tag in `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`.
- Replace current MIT `LICENSE` with `AGPL-3.0-or-later` for GridWars code (preserve Evennia BSD-3 inside vendor tree).
- Add SPDX headers convention note for new source files.
- Expand `.gitignore`: `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `.venv/`, `venv/`, `*.sqlite3`, `*.db3`, `server/logs/`, `server/*.pid`, `.coverage`, `htmlcov/`, `.pytest_cache/`, `*.egg-info/`, `.env`, `.env.local`.
- Add `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` acknowledging Evennia BSD-3-Clause + listing upstream version.

## Acceptance
- `vendor/evennia` exists, is a submodule pinned to a stable tag, and `git submodule status` is clean.
- `LICENSE` is AGPL-3.0-or-later for GridWars code.
- `vendor/evennia/LICENSE.txt` (Evennia's BSD-3) is preserved untouched.
- `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` lists Evennia upstream URL, tag, license.
- `.gitignore` covers everything in Scope.
- `git status` clean after submodule init on a fresh clone.

## Model recommendation
- **Researcher (Claude haiku)** — look up latest Evennia stable tag + confirm vendoring approach.
- **Builder (Codex)** — Python/git submodule mechanics, gitignore patterns.
- **Writer (Claude haiku)** — license + attribution prose.

## Notes
- Do NOT modify anything inside `vendor/evennia` — game code goes elsewhere.
- License change is a deliberate decision worth surfacing in the commit message.
- Blocks: Epic 2 (Bootstrap), Epic 10 (Docs).
close_reason: Epic 1 (Foundation) complete: vendored Evennia, AGPL license, notices, SPDX docs, gitignore, verified clean-clone procedure

title: [T10.1] README.md — pitch, setup, commands, roadmap
description: Write a README.md that survives the GitHub front page. Sections: (1) hero with logo/banner — link to gridwars-banner.svg in repo root; (2) tagline "Full PvP. No limits. Free to play. Open source."; (3) one-paragraph pitch (cyber-grid, terminal-native, programs, daemons); (4) "Get on the Grid" — clone+submodule, make install, make migrate, make run, telnet/web URLs; (5) "Whats vendored" — vendor/evennia v6.0.0 BSD-3 link to THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md; (6) License — AGPL-3.0-or-later, link LICENSE; (7) "First playable slice" — table of in-game commands (status/scan/faction/strike/help gridwars); (8) Roadmap tease — lightcycle duels, daemon AI, sector ownership; (9) badge for CI (link to workflow when T9.1 lands). Reference docs/SETUP.md for full setup detail. Acceptance: renders cleanly on GitHub; all linked files exist; setup steps work on fresh clone.

title: [T10.2] CONTRIBUTING.md — workflow + don't touch vendor/
description: Write CONTRIBUTING.md with: how to file issues (bd/GitHub); PR workflow (branch from main, run make test, sign off if AGPL/DCO chosen); code style (PEP 8, ruff if added); the absolute rule "DO NOT edit vendor/evennia/"; how to add new commands (Evennia idiom + cmdset registration); how to extend factions/world (point at world/factions.py + world/build_grid.py); SPDX header convention (link docs/CONVENTIONS.md). Short — under 200 lines. Acceptance: renders on GitHub; covers issue filing + PR steps + the vendor rule.

title: [T9.1] GitHub Actions CI workflow + coverage gate
description: Create .github/workflows/test.yml that runs `make test` on push and PR against main. Matrix: single entry on Python 3.12 + ubuntu-latest (multi-version expansion is future work). Steps: checkout w/ submodules, setup Python 3.12, make install, make migrate, make test. Cache .venv/ between runs (cache key = hash of pyproject.toml + vendor/evennia commit). Annotate PR with results on failure. Acceptance: `gh run list --workflow=test.yml` shows green on push to a feature branch.

title: [T9.2] Fill test gaps: factions + status + scan + world build
description: Add unit tests for the playable-slice commands not yet covered. Files: gridwars/commands/tests/test_factions.py (faction list + faction choose validation + re-choose refusal), gridwars/commands/tests/test_status.py (HUD renders all 7 fields + faction color), gridwars/commands/tests/test_scan.py (other players visible + faction tint + self-excluded + exits listed + flavor fallback), gridwars/world/tests/test_build_grid.py (5 rooms + idempotent rerun + all tagged + all 8 exits). Use EvenniaCommandTest pattern from C3/St2. Acceptance: total `make test` count grows from 14 to ~25+, all green.

title: [fix] AttributeProperty access via .attributes.get() returns None — switch to direct attr access
description: I1 (PR #22) discovered: `caller.attributes.get("faction")` returns None for AttributeProperty-backed fields because AttributeProperty creates Python descriptors that bypass the .attributes.get() API. Direct access (`caller.faction`) is the correct idiom. Sweep the codebase and fix every occurrence in commands. Known offenders: gridwars/commands/factions.py (CmdFaction._do_list and CmdFaction._do_choose check `caller.attributes.get("faction")` — silently always treats user as unaffiliated), and likely gridwars/commands/scan.py once I2 (PR pending) lands. Acceptance: grep `attributes.get(.*faction|integrity|energy|experience|grid_rank.*)` in gridwars/commands/ returns zero matches; all stat reads use direct attribute access; F1 list view correctly shows current affiliation after `faction choose`; in-game smoke test (manual or via T6-style boot) confirms faction display.
close_reason: shipped via PR #26; also closes implicit wave-5 cmdset-registration deferral

title: [W3-redo] Fix START_LOCATION dbref: #2 is Lightcycle Causeway, not Users' Sector
description:
# W3 verification: dbref mismatch — spawn lands in Lightcycle Causeway

**Verification run completed on Epic 3 W3.** Steps 1-5 passed cleanly:
- `make install` + `make migrate` succeeded
- `world.build_grid` created 5 sectors + 8 exits on first run
- Tag query confirmed 5 rooms tagged `("gridwars-core","world_build")`
- Second run confirmed idempotency (still exactly 5 rooms)

**Step 6 (spawn-check) FAILED due to dbref mismatch.**

`settings.py` has `START_LOCATION = "#2"`. After `world.build_grid` runs, the sector dbrefs are:
- `#1` — Users' Sector (correct spawn target)
- `#2` — Lightcycle Causeway (actual spawn location — WRONG)
- `#3` — Daemon Gate
- `#4` — Archive Node
- `#5` — Combat Grid

A fresh character connecting as `testuser2` spawned in **Lightcycle Causeway** (confirmed via telnet look output). The fix is to change `START_LOCATION` from `"#2"` to `"#1"` in `gridwars/server/conf/settings.py`.

**Owned by W2-followup (gridwars_run-8gg.4).** Per the handoff discipline, W3-tester does NOT modify `settings.py`.

**Resolution:** Update `START_LOCATION = "#2"` → `START_LOCATION = "#1"` in settings.py. Re-verify spawn with a fresh character after the fix.
notes: PR #19 (epic3/w2-followup-spawn-hook) exists with the tag-lookup fix but is OPEN and not merged to main. W3 ran against main. Once PR #19 merges, re-run steps 6-7 to close Epic 3.
close_reason: re-verified post W2-followup; spawn now correct — character lands in Users' Sector (#1) via Account.create_character tag hook

title: [W2-followup] Runtime-safe START_LOCATION via tag lookup (not dbref)
description: PR #15 (W2+C2 batch) set `START_LOCATION = "#2"` because Evennia v6 accounts.py:962 passes the value directly to `ObjectDB.objects.get_id()` which rejects callables. The dbref "#2" is only correct if Users Sector is the first room build_grid creates AND no Limbo exists — likely false in practice (Limbo is #2 by default after migrate). The robust fix: implement an at_account_creation hook on a Account subclass (or override the existing Character spawn logic) that calls `search_tag("users_sector", category="world_build")` to find the live dbref at character-creation time. Acceptance: a fresh character spawned post-build_grid lands in Users Sector regardless of dbref assignment order. Discovered-from: gridwars_run-8gg.2.
close_reason: shipped via PR #19 (epic3/w2-followup-spawn-hook, ef0c8db)

title: [I1] 'status' command — neon HUD of caller stats
description: Create gridwars/commands/status.py with `status` command. Renders a small ANSI/neon-grid table: character name, current sector (caller.location.key), faction (or |yunaffiliated|n), integrity/100, energy/100, experience, grid_rank. Style: |c headers, |g good stats, |y warnings (low integrity), |r criticals (<25% integrity). Use Evennia color codes only — no raw ANSI escapes. Register on default character cmdset. Docstring usable via `help status`. Acceptance: in-game `status` displays all 7 fields with live values; renders correctly in both telnet AND webclient.
close_reason: shipped via PR #22; cmdset registration deferred to follow-up wave-5 consolidation PR

title: [I2] 'scan' command — players + exits + sector flavor
description: Create gridwars/commands/scan.py with `scan` command. Lists: (a) other Characters in caller.location (exclude self; name + faction color if set), (b) visible exits (name + destination if visible), (c) sector flavor — pulled from caller.location.db.scan_flavor (Attribute) if set, else first sentence of caller.location.db.desc as a fallback. Compact output, neon-grid voice. Register on cmdset. `help scan` returns docstring. Acceptance: scan from a populated room omits self, lists others with faction tint, lists exits, prints the flavor line.
close_reason: shipped via PR #24; cmdset registration deferred to follow-up PR

title: [St1] combat.py constants + 'strike <target>' command + defeat/respawn
description: Create gridwars/world/combat.py with tunable constants (BASE_DAMAGE=10, RANDOM_BONUS_MAX=5, EXP_ON_VICTORY=5, RESPAWN_INTEGRITY=25). Create gridwars/commands/combat.py with `strike <target>` command. Resolution: target = caller.search(target, candidates=caller.location.contents) (Evennia idiom); refuse self-strike, refuse non-Character target, refuse out-of-room (search returns nothing → error). Damage = BASE_DAMAGE + randint(0, RANDOM_BONUS_MAX). Call target.take_damage(amount) (Epic 4 C1 helper). Messages: attacker ("You strike {target} for {n} integrity."), defender ("{attacker} strikes you for {n} integrity."), room ("{attacker} strikes {target}!"). On target.integrity == 0 after the hit: defeat flow → room broadcast ("{target} derezzes — sectors recycle the pattern."), target.location = Users-Sector (resolve via tag lookup not hardcoded dbref), target.reset_for_respawn(min_integrity=RESPAWN_INTEGRITY), attacker.gain_experience(EXP_ON_VICTORY). Target is NEVER deleted. Register strike on default character cmdset. `help strike` returns docstring.
close_reason: shipped via PR #23; default_cmdsets.py registration deferred to wave-5 follow-up

title: [St2] Unit tests for strike: happy path + 4 failure modes + defeat→respawn
description: Add gridwars/commands/tests/test_strike.py using Evennia EvenniaCommandTest base. Cover: (1) same-room hit reduces target.integrity by [BASE_DAMAGE, BASE_DAMAGE+RANDOM_BONUS_MAX]; (2) cross-room strike fails with clear error (target not found); (3) self-strike refused; (4) strike on non-Character (e.g., a room or exit) refused; (5) strike that brings target.integrity to 0 triggers defeat: target.location moves to Users Sector, target.integrity == RESPAWN_INTEGRITY, attacker.experience grows by EXP_ON_VICTORY, target NOT deleted (still exists in DB). Acceptance: all 5 tests pass via `make test`.
close_reason: shipped via PR #28; 5 strike tests pass (14 total with character suite)

title: [F1] Faction registry + 'faction' command (list)
description: Create gridwars/world/factions.py with the 3 founding factions: Users (civilian programs, default underdog, color cyan, tagline "Uploaded. Underestimated."), Programs (entrenched grid natives, color green, tagline "Order is the throughput."), Daemons (rogue/corrupted processes, color red, tagline "We are the noise in your stack."). Each: name, tagline, color (Evennia |c-style code), 2-3 sentence description. Then create gridwars/commands/factions.py with `faction` command (no args) that renders the registry as a neon ANSI table, indicating the callers current affiliation (or "unaffiliated"). Register the command on the default character cmdset. Acceptance: in-game `faction` lists all 3 factions with descriptions, shows callers current affiliation (None initially); `help faction` returns the docstring.
close_reason: shipped via PR #18, commit fdbb572b

title: [F2] 'faction choose <name>' command + broadcast
description: Add `faction choose <name>` subcommand to the existing faction command from F1 (one-shot for MVP — set once, admin-override only). Validate name against registry (case-insensitive match); on success: set caller.faction = "<CanonicalName>" (Character.faction is Epic 4 C1 territory — just write the string), broadcast a themed join message to caller.location ("|c{name}|n has uploaded into the |g{faction}|n."). On invalid name: print available options. Refuse re-choose if already affiliated (print current + "use admin override to change"). Acceptance: `faction choose Users` sets affiliation persistently across restart; invalid name errors cleanly; same-room observers see the broadcast.
close_reason: shipped via PR #20 (epic5/f2-faction-choose, SHA 07bc89f)

title: [Th1] connection_screens.py: GridWars banner + MOTD
description: Replace gridwars/server/conf/connection_screens.py with GridWars-themed content. CONNECTION_SCREEN: ASCII art banner (max 80 cols, terminal-safe), tagline "Full PvP. No limits. Free to play. Open source.", version stamp. MOTD: 3-6 lines, neon-grid voice (terminals, programs, identity discs, the Grid). Acceptance: fresh telnet connect shows the GridWars banner instead of Evennia default; copy renders correctly in both telnet and webclient (ANSI degrades).
close_reason: shipped via PR #13 (branch epic8/th1-connection-screen, SHA fc75643)

title: [Th2] gridwars/world/messages.py + login-hook templates
description: Create gridwars/world/messages.py centralizing themed system messages: login ("Identity disc verified. Welcome back, |c{name}|n."), logout ("Disconnecting from the Grid..."), faction-unaffiliated nudge (shown once per session if caller.faction is None). Wire into Character at_post_login (or similar Evennia hook) for login/logout. Faction-nudge wiring may need to wait for Epic 5 to expose caller.faction — if so, leave the message defined but defer the hook to a follow-up bd. Acceptance: login message renders themed; logout shows the disconnect line; no inline strings scattered through other modules.
close_reason: shipped via PR #16

title: [C2] Wire BASE_CHARACTER_TYPECLASS in settings.py
description: Edit gridwars/server/conf/settings.py to add BASE_CHARACTER_TYPECLASS = "typeclasses.characters.Character" (path is relative to the gridwars/ game dir, per Evennia convention). Verify the value resolves: `from evennia.utils.utils import class_from_module; class_from_module(BASE_CHARACTER_TYPECLASS)` returns the C1 subclass. Acceptance: new characters created by Evennia use the GridWars Character subclass (verify via in-game @typeclass or by inspecting char.attributes after creation).
close_reason: shipped via PR #15 (epic3-4/w2-c2-settings-wire, SHA b0dc523)

title: [C3] Unit tests for Character stats + helpers
description: Add gridwars/typeclasses/tests/test_characters.py using Evennia EvenniaTest base class. Cover: default values on creation, take_damage clamping (negative input rejected? overflow to 0?), heal clamping (cap respected), gain_experience monotonicity, reset_for_respawn restores integrity floor without zeroing other stats, faction defaults to None. ~6-8 focused tests. Acceptance: `make test` runs them green. NO mock DB — use Evennia test base class which spins a real one.
close_reason: shipped via PR #17; 9 tests passing

title: [W3] Verify idempotent rebuild + connectivity graph
description: Test the World epic end-to-end. (1) Wipe DB (or use a scratch test DB), run migrate + build_grid, count rooms tagged gridwars-core = 5. (2) Run build_grid AGAIN, count is still 5 (no duplicates). (3) From Users Sector verify reachability to all 4 other sectors via the documented exits. (4) Append a "Building the grid" subsection to docs/SETUP.md with the exact command + the connectivity diagram. Acceptance: all checks pass; docs updated. Closes Epic 3 on PR merge.
close_reason: Verification run complete: steps 1-5 pass (5 rooms built, idempotent, 8 exits), step 6 FAILED — START_LOCATION='#2' points to Lightcycle Causeway not Users' Sector; redo filed as gridwars_run-40t

title: [C1] Character typeclass with persistent stats + helpers
description: Create gridwars/typeclasses/characters.py subclassing evennia.DefaultCharacter. Persistent Attributes via AttributeProperty (preferred — auto-DB-backed, type-safe; fall back to db.* getter/setter if AttributeProperty has issues in v6.0.0): integrity (int, default 100, clamp [0,100]), energy (int, default 50, clamp [0,100]), experience (int, default 0, clamp [0, inf)), faction (str|None, default None — Epic 5 sets), grid_rank (str, default "User"). Helpers: take_damage(amount: int) -> int (returns new integrity, clamps to 0), heal(amount: int, cap: int|None=None), gain_experience(amount: int), reset_for_respawn(min_integrity: int = 25) (restore integrity floor, keep other stats). at_object_creation hook initializes all defaults atomically. NO faction validation in this file — Epic 5 owns that. NO combat damage formula — Epic 6 owns that. Acceptance: import works, AttributeProperty round-trips, helpers clamp correctly.
close_reason: shipped: PR #12 https://github.com/jsgerman-oss/gridwars.run/pull/12

title: [W1] Build script: 5 sectors + exits with gridwars-core tag
description: Create gridwars/world/build_grid.py — an Evennia build script that creates the 5 named sectors and connects them with cardinal exits. Sectors: Users Sector (spawn), Lightcycle Causeway, Daemon Gate, Archive Node, Combat Grid. Connectivity: Users Sector <-> Lightcycle Causeway (n/s), Lightcycle Causeway <-> Daemon Gate (e/w), Users Sector <-> Archive Node (e/w), Daemon Gate <-> Combat Grid (n/s). Each room: 3-6 sentence neon-grid flavor (programs, light trails, daemons, identity discs). Tag every room with key="gridwars-core" so rebuild is idempotent (wipe-tagged-then-recreate, OR skip-if-exists). Acceptance: `evennia batchcmd world.build_grid` from gridwars/ creates all 5 rooms; second run produces no duplicates; `evennia objects --tag gridwars-core` lists exactly 5 rooms after either run.
close_reason: shipped via PR #14

title: [W2] Wire START_LOCATION to Users' Sector dbref
description: After W1 creates Users Sector, edit gridwars/server/conf/settings.py to set START_LOCATION to the Users Sector dbref. Two valid patterns: (a) hardcode the dbref (e.g. "#2" — fragile if rebuild changes ordering), or (b) use Evennia lookup-by-tag at runtime via a tiny wrapper. Prefer (b) — set START_LOCATION via a deferred string that resolves "first room tagged gridwars-core where key==Users Sector". This keeps the wiring robust across rebuilds. Acceptance: a freshly-created character spawns in Users Sector (not Limbo). Verify by inspecting evennia.search_tag or via in-game.
close_reason: shipped via PR #15 (epic3-4/w2-c2-settings-wire, SHA b0dc523)

title: [T5] Pin dependencies in pyproject.toml at repo root
description: Create root-level pyproject.toml pinning Evennia at the vendored version (path-dep on vendor/evennia OR version-pinned to 6.0.0). Include project metadata: name=gridwars, version=0.1.0, requires-python=">=3.12", license={text="AGPL-3.0-or-later"}, dependencies=["evennia @ file://./vendor/evennia"] (or per T1 recommendation). This file is the lockfile-equivalent for the bootstrap. Acceptance: `.venv/bin/pip install -e .` from repo root resolves and installs Evennia at v6.0.0 from the vendored path.
close_reason: shipped via PR #9

title: [T6] Verify boot end-to-end + document run procedure
description: End-to-end verification + docs append. (1) `make install && make migrate && make run` brings up the server. (2) Confirm telnet on `localhost:4000` accepts a connection (nc/expect short interaction). (3) Confirm webclient on `http://localhost:4001` serves an HTTP 200 with the Evennia welcome page (curl head check). (4) `make stop` cleanly halts the server. (5) `make test` runs Evennia test harness against gridwars/ (may be empty, must not error). (6) Append a "Running GridWars.run locally" section to docs/SETUP.md covering install / migrate / run / stop / test. Acceptance: all five command checks pass in PR body; SETUP.md updated; closes Epic 2 (gridwars_run-th6).
close_reason: shipped via PR #11; 8/8 acceptance checks passed; Makefile gamedir bug also fixed

title: [T3] Makefile with install/migrate/run/stop/test helpers
description: Add a root-level Makefile so contributors run a one-word command rather than memorize the evennia CLI. Targets: `make install` (create venv + install vendored Evennia per T2), `make migrate` (Django/Evennia DB migrations), `make run` (start the server), `make stop` (stop), `make test` (run Evennia test harness). Each target invokes `.venv/bin/evennia` or `.venv/bin/python -m evennia` as appropriate. Idempotent: `make install` on an existing venv must not error. Acceptance: `make help` lists all targets with one-line descriptions; each target works against a fresh clone after T2 is done.
close_reason: shipped via PR #8

title: [T4] Tune gridwars/server/conf/settings.py for GridWars.run
description: Customize the Evennia settings.py generated by T2 to brand it as GridWars.run. Changes: SERVERNAME="GridWars.run"; confirm DEBUG=False default for prod profile (dev profile can override via env); telnet port stays 4000; web port stays 4001 (Evennia defaults). DO NOT modify anything inside vendor/evennia. Acceptance: `grep SERVERNAME gridwars/server/conf/settings.py` shows GridWars.run; `evennia start` (T6 will run this) shows the new name in the boot banner.
close_reason: shipped via PR #10

title: [S7] Verify clean-clone + submodule init workflow
description:
# Story — Verify clean-clone + submodule workflow

## Why
A vendored submodule that only works on the original developer's machine is a trap. Before closing Epic 1, prove that a fresh clone from `origin` reproduces a working tree: submodule populates, license files are present, `.gitignore` masks runtime debris correctly, and no untracked files appear from a noop checkout.

## Scope
- In a scratch directory (`/tmp/gridwars-verify-<timestamp>`):
  - `git clone --recurse-submodules <origin-url> gridwars-clone`
  - `cd gridwars-clone && git submodule status`
  - Verify `vendor/evennia/LICENSE.txt` exists and contains BSD-3-Clause.
  - Verify root `LICENSE` is AGPL-3.0-or-later.
  - Verify `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` exists and references Evennia + correct tag/SHA.
  - Run `git status` — must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".
  - Touch a file that SHOULD be ignored (e.g. `server/logs/foo.log`, `dummy.sqlite3`, `.coverage`) — verify `git status` still clean.
- Document the verification procedure in `docs/SETUP.md` (or inline in README pending Epic 10).
- File a follow-up bd if any acceptance fails.

## Acceptance
- Fresh-clone verification passes all six checks above.
- Procedure documented for future contributors.
- No untracked files appear on a noop checkout.

## Dependencies
- S2 (vendor), S3 (LICENSE swap), S4 (notices), S5 (gitignore), S6 (SPDX note) all done.

## Suggested platform
Codex tester — bash verification, gh CLI fluency.
close_reason: shipped via PR #6; all 7 acceptance checks passed on fresh /tmp/ clone

title: [S5] Expand .gitignore for Python/Evennia/SQLite runtime
description: Append Python/Evennia/SQLite/test/secret patterns to .gitignore. PRESERVE existing entries (.beads/, .dolt/, *.db, .beads-credential-key). Add: `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `*.pyo`, `*.pyd`, `.venv/`, `venv/`, `env/`, `*.sqlite3`, `*.db3`, `server/logs/`, `server/*.pid`, `server/*.restart`, `.coverage`, `htmlcov/`, `.pytest_cache/`, `.mypy_cache/`, `.ruff_cache/`, `*.egg-info/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `.env`, `.env.local`, `.DS_Store`. Acceptance: `git check-ignore -v dummy.pyc server/logs/foo.log .coverage .env` resolves all four to .gitignore lines; existing beads/dolt entries untouched.
close_reason: shipped via PR #3

title: [S4] Add THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md with Evennia BSD-3 attribution
description:
# Story — Add THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md

## Why
AGPL projects that vendor BSD/MIT/Apache code must preserve attribution. THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md is the canonical place to do this — a single file listing every third-party component, its license, version, and source. Required for license compliance and good open-source citizenship; also future-proofs the project against forgotten dependencies.

## Scope
Create `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` at repo root with:

- A short intro paragraph: "GridWars.run is licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later. It vendors and depends on the following third-party components, whose licenses and copyrights are reproduced or referenced below."
- An entry for **Evennia**:
  - Component: Evennia
  - Upstream: https://github.com/evennia/evennia
  - Pinned tag: `<from S1>`
  - Pinned commit: `<from S2>`
  - License: BSD-3-Clause
  - License file: `vendor/evennia/LICENSE.txt` (reference, not duplicate)
  - One-sentence purpose: "MU* engine providing networking, sessions, typeclasses, web client."
- A "Future entries" template comment in HTML so contributors know the shape to copy.

## Acceptance
- File exists at repo root.
- Evennia entry includes tag, commit, license name, license file reference.
- README (when written in Epic 10) can link to this file for the license-attribution section.
- File renders cleanly on GitHub.

## Dependencies
- S1 (Evennia research) — needs the tag.
- S2 (vendoring) — needs the committed submodule SHA.

## Suggested platform
Claude haiku writer — short structured prose, no code.
close_reason: shipped via PR #5 (foundation/s4-third-party-notices, commit 8400598)

title: [EPIC] Tests & Verification Harness
description:
# Epic 9 — Tests & Verification Harness

## Why
This is an open-source project that other people will contribute to. Without tests, every refactor is a coin flip. Evennia ships its own test framework based on Django's `TestCase`; we should adopt it from day one and gate merges on it green. Scope here is the playable slice — character stats, faction validation, combat damage/defeat, status/scan output. CI scaffolding is in-scope but minimal (GitHub Actions, single matrix entry).

## Scope
- Adopt Evennia's `EvenniaTest` / `EvenniaCommandTest` base classes.
- Test files under `gridwars/typeclasses/tests/`, `gridwars/commands/tests/`, `gridwars/world/tests/`.
- Coverage targets (not %; specific scenarios):
  - **Character**: defaults, take_damage clamping, heal clamping, gain_experience, reset_for_respawn.
  - **Faction**: list, valid choose, invalid choose, persistence across stat read.
  - **Strike**: same-room hit, cross-room rejection, self-strike rejection, non-character rejection, defeat → respawn to Users' Sector, attacker gains exp.
  - **Status / Scan**: status renders all fields, scan omits self, scan lists exits.
  - **World build**: idempotent rebuild produces 5 rooms tagged "gridwars-core".
- CI: `.github/workflows/test.yml` runs `make test` on push/PR against `main`.
- `make test` wraps `evennia test --settings settings.py gridwars`.

## Acceptance
- `make test` runs green locally.
- GitHub Actions runs green on push to a feature branch.
- Every command in the playable slice has at least one happy-path and one failure-path test.
- Test names follow `test_<unit>_<scenario>` convention.

## Model recommendation
- **Tester (Codex)** — pytest/Django test fluency; Evennia test classes.
- **Builder (Codex)** — `make test` + CI YAML.
- **Reviewer (Claude sonnet)** — test-quality pass (are tests testing behavior, not implementation?).

## Notes
- Depends on Epics 4, 5, 6, 7 having shipped their code (tests can be authored against the spec ahead of code if TDD'd).
- Future: coverage thresholds, mutation testing, fuzz tests on combat.

title: [EPIC] Player Info Commands (Status, Scan, Help)
description:
# Epic 7 — Player Info Commands (Status, Scan, Help)

## Why
Players need to read their own state and survey their surroundings. `status` is the player's HUD; `scan` is the player's tactical view of the room. Without these, players can't make any informed decision — they can't see their integrity before a fight, can't see who else is in the Combat Grid, can't tell what exits exist beyond `look`. Help integration ensures the entire command surface is self-discoverable.

## Scope
- Command `status`:
  - Renders a small ANSI/markdown table with: character name, current sector (room name), faction (or "unaffiliated"), integrity/max, energy/max, experience, grid_rank.
  - Visual style: cyan/neon palette using Evennia's color codes (|c, |g, |r, etc.).
- Command `scan`:
  - Lists visible characters in the room (excluding self).
  - Lists visible exits.
  - Reprints a one-line "sector flavor" pulled from the room's `db.scan_flavor` attribute (fallback: first sentence of room description).
- Help integration:
  - `help status`, `help scan`, `help strike`, `help faction` all return useful text via command docstrings.
  - Add a top-level `help gridwars` entry pointing to the curated command list.

## Acceptance
- `status` displays all fields with current values.
- `scan` lists other characters + exits + flavor, omits self.
- `help <cmd>` works for the four commands listed.
- `help gridwars` returns a curated index.
- Commands render correctly in both telnet and webclient.

## Model recommendation
- **Builder (Codex)** — Evennia Command + ANSI color codes.
- **Writer (Claude haiku)** — help index copy + sector flavor fallback strings.

## Notes
- Depends on Epic 3 (rooms), Epic 4 (stats).
- Future: `who` (cross-room player list), `where` (sector map ASCII art), `inventory`.
close_reason: Epic 7 (Player Info Commands) complete: status (PR #22), scan (PR #23), help index (PR #25)

title: [EPIC] Theming, MOTD & Welcome Copy
description:
# Epic 8 — Theming, MOTD & Welcome Copy

## Why
First impressions matter. The default Evennia welcome screen screams "tutorial MUD". GridWars promises a neon, terminal-native, factional digital battleground — the player's first 30 seconds need to deliver that. This is the highest-leverage prose work in the project: connection banner, MOTD, login flow copy, faction-onboarding nudge, and templated system messages.

## Scope
- Replace `gridwars/server/conf/connection_screens.py` with a custom GridWars banner: ASCII art (small, terminal-safe), tagline ("Full PvP. No limits. Free to play. Open source."), version stamp.
- Replace MOTD with GridWars-themed copy (3–6 lines max, neon-grid voice).
- Custom login / character-creation prompts: "Specify your handle. The Grid is listening."
- Templated system messages in `gridwars/world/messages.py`:
  - Login: "Identity disc verified. Welcome back, |c{name}|n."
  - Logout: "Disconnecting from the Grid…"
  - Faction-unaffiliated nudge: shown after login if `caller.faction is None`.
- Ensure all themed copy renders correctly in telnet AND webclient (ANSI codes degrade cleanly).

## Acceptance
- Fresh telnet connect shows the GridWars banner + MOTD instead of Evennia defaults.
- Login message uses the themed template.
- Unaffiliated players see the faction-nudge once per session after login.
- All copy is centralized in `gridwars/world/messages.py` and `connection_screens.py` (no inline strings scattered through commands).

## Model recommendation
- **Writer (Claude sonnet)** — prose, banner copy, voice consistency. This is the strongest writing task in the slice.
- **Builder (Codex)** — connection screen + login-hook wiring.

## Notes
- Banner must be ≤ 80 cols wide for old telnet clients.
- Future: per-faction MOTDs, dynamic "current grid status" line.
- Depends on Epic 2 (Bootstrap). Can run in parallel with Epics 3–7.
close_reason: Epic 8 (Theming, MOTD & Welcome Copy) complete: Th1 connection screen (PR #13), Th2 messages.py + login hooks (PR #16)

title: [EPIC] Faction System
description:
# Epic 5 — Faction System

## Why
Factions are a core identity hook for the GridWars fiction (Users / Programs / Daemons). They drive future systems (PvP rivalries, sector access, daemon AI behavior, ranking) and onboarding flavor. The MVP is small: a registry of three factions, a command to view available factions and your current one, and a command to choose your faction (once, or with a cooldown — TBD by design choice in the story breakdown).

## Scope
- Faction registry in `gridwars/world/factions.py`:
  - `Users` — civilian programs uploaded into the grid; default underdog faction.
  - `Programs` — entrenched grid natives; structured combat.
  - `Daemons` — rogue/corrupted processes; chaotic, fast.
  - Each entry: `name`, `tagline`, `color`, `description`.
- Command `faction` (no args) — show list of factions and current affiliation.
- Command `faction choose <name>` — set `caller.faction`, validate against registry, broadcast a join message.
- Persistent on Character (Epic 4 already stores `faction`).
- Help docstrings render under `help faction`.
- (Design decision for breakdown: faction-change cooldown vs. one-shot. Suggest one-shot for MVP with an admin override.)

## Acceptance
- `faction` lists three factions with descriptions and shows the caller's current faction (or "unaffiliated").
- `faction choose Users` sets the affiliation, persists across restart.
- Invalid `faction choose Foo` errors cleanly with the valid options.
- Same-room observers see a themed "X has joined the <Faction>" message.
- `help faction` returns useful docstring.

## Model recommendation
- **Builder (Codex)** — Evennia Command class, command set wiring.
- **Writer (Claude sonnet)** — faction taglines + join broadcast copy.
- **Reviewer (Claude sonnet)** — code review for command idioms + edge cases.

## Notes
- Depends on Epic 4 (Character stores `faction`).
- Future: per-faction starting kit, sector access, leaderboard scoping.
- Blocks: nothing hard, but Epic 6 may want faction-aware combat messaging.
close_reason: Epic 5 (Faction System) complete: registry + list + choose + broadcast — PR #20

title: [EPIC] PvP Combat (Strike)
description:
# Epic 6 — PvP Combat (Strike)

## Why
The promise is "Full PvP. No limits." The simplest possible PvP primitive that earns that line: target a same-room opponent, deal damage, and on defeat send them back to spawn alive but humbled. Permanent character deletion is OFF the table — this game is about the next round, not data loss. The strike command is the smallest interactive PvP loop and the foundation for every later combat system.

## Scope
- Command: `strike <target>`
  - Resolves `<target>` to a Character in `caller.location` (case-insensitive, partial match OK).
  - Refuses self-strike, refuses non-Character targets, refuses out-of-room.
  - Damage: deterministic floor (e.g. 10) + small random bonus (e.g. `randint(0, 5)`). Number lives in a constant for easy tuning.
  - Calls `target.take_damage(amount)` (Epic 4 helper).
  - Messages: attacker (`You strike X for N integrity.`), target (`X strikes you for N integrity.`), room (`X strikes Y!`).
  - On `target.integrity <= 0`: trigger defeat flow.
- Defeat flow:
  - Broadcast room message (themed: derez / overload / etc.).
  - `target.location = Users' Sector` (use `START_LOCATION` lookup, not a hardcoded #dbref).
  - `target.reset_for_respawn()` (Epic 4 helper) — restores integrity to a min (e.g. 25), zeroes nothing else.
  - Attacker `caller.gain_experience(amount=5)`.
  - Defeated character is NOT deleted.
- All numeric constants live in `gridwars/world/combat.py` (no magic numbers in command file).
- Help docstring usable via `help strike`.

## Acceptance
- Two characters in the same room can `strike` each other.
- Damage applies, defeat triggers respawn to Users' Sector with restored integrity.
- Strike across rooms fails with a clear error.
- Strike on self / non-character target fails with a clear error.
- Attacker gains experience on victory.
- No characters are deleted under any circumstance.
- Unit tests (Epic 9) cover the happy path + 4 failure modes.

## Model recommendation
- **Architect (Claude opus)** — quick design review of damage formula + defeat semantics (load-bearing for future combat).
- **Builder (Codex)** — Evennia Command implementation.
- **Tester (Codex)** — strike-flow tests including respawn assertions.

## Notes
- Depends on Epic 3 (rooms exist), Epic 4 (stats + helpers exist).
- Future: factions affect damage modifiers, energy as a resource cost, cooldowns, weapon equip.
- Blocks: nothing — but Epic 9 needs this implemented to write the combat tests.
close_reason: Epic 6 (PvP Combat) complete: combat.py + strike + defeat → respawn + 5 tests (PR #23, #28)

title: [EPIC] World: Sectors & Connectivity
description:
# Epic 3 — World: Sectors & Connectivity

## Why
GridWars needs a place. The first playable slice promises five named sectors with grid-flavored prose and clear exits, plus a defined spawn point. Without rooms, every other system (combat, scan, status, faction tutorials) has nowhere to live. Building these via an idempotent `world/batch_build` script (Evennia's `@batchcommand` or a build script) means devs can rebuild a fresh world deterministically.

## Scope
- Create five rooms: **Users' Sector** (spawn), **Lightcycle Causeway**, **Daemon Gate**, **Archive Node**, **Combat Grid**.
- Each room has neon-grid flavor text (~3–6 sentences, theme: terminals, programs, light trails, daemons).
- Connect with cardinal/grid-style exits (`north`/`south`/`east`/`west`, plus aliases like `n`/`s`/`e`/`w`).
- Make Users' Sector the default `START_LOCATION`.
- Provide a build script: `gridwars/world/build_grid.py` that wipes prior build artifacts (tagged) and re-creates rooms idempotently.
- Tag rooms with `tag="gridwars-core"` so the build is rerunnable without orphaning duplicates.

## Acceptance
- After `make run`, a new account spawns into Users' Sector.
- All five rooms exist and `look` shows themed descriptions.
- Each room is reachable from at least one other room.
- Re-running `python -m evennia migrate && evennia run batchcommand world.build_grid` does not create duplicates.
- `tag="gridwars-core"` set on every built room.

## Model recommendation
- **Builder (Codex)** — Evennia typeclass + batchcmd idioms, Python.
- **Writer (Claude sonnet)** — sector flavor prose (or hand off to Epic 8 if Inoshiro/Hashim is already in the copy loop).

## Notes
- Connectivity graph (suggested):
  - Users' Sector ↔ Lightcycle Causeway (north/south)
  - Lightcycle Causeway ↔ Daemon Gate (east/west)
  - Users' Sector ↔ Archive Node (east/west)
  - Daemon Gate ↔ Combat Grid (north/south)
- Combat Grid is the intended PvP arena hint for Epic 6.
- Blocks: Epic 6 (Strike needs same-room targets), Epic 7 (Scan needs sectors).
close_reason: Epic 3 (World) complete: 5 sectors built+verified, spawn lands in Users' Sector, SETUP.md updated

title: [EPIC] Character Typeclass & Persistent Stats
description:
# Epic 4 — Character Typeclass & Persistent Stats

## Why
Every other gameplay system depends on character state: combat reads/writes integrity, faction commands set affiliation, status reads everything, scan reads visible character data. The stats need to be persistent (Evennia Attributes, not in-memory), bounded, and have safe defaults so a freshly-created character never crashes a command. Defining this layer once, cleanly, prevents per-feature stat hacks.

## Scope
- Subclass `evennia.DefaultCharacter` → `gridwars.typeclasses.characters.Character`.
- Persistent Attributes (via `AttributeProperty` where possible):
  - `integrity` (int, default 100, min 0, max 100)
  - `energy` (int, default 50, min 0, max 100)
  - `experience` (int, default 0, min 0)
  - `faction` (str, default `None` — unset)
  - `grid_rank` (str or int, default `"User"` or 1)
- Initialization hook (`at_object_creation`) sets all defaults atomically.
- Helper methods: `take_damage(amount)`, `heal(amount, cap=None)`, `gain_experience(amount)`, `reset_for_respawn()`.
- All stat changes go through helpers (avoid scattered `obj.db.integrity -= n` calls).
- Wire as the default character typeclass in `settings.py`: `BASE_CHARACTER_TYPECLASS = "typeclasses.characters.Character"`.

## Acceptance
- New characters start with the documented defaults.
- Stats persist across `evennia stop && evennia start`.
- Helper methods clamp to min/max correctly (unit tested in Epic 9).
- `BASE_CHARACTER_TYPECLASS` resolves to our subclass.
- Existing default characters (if any) migrate cleanly (or are explicitly wiped + recreated for dev).

## Model recommendation
- **Builder (Codex)** — Evennia typeclass + AttributeProperty patterns.
- **Tester (Codex)** — write the unit tests in tandem (TDD).

## Notes
- Keep helpers small and pure where possible; combat math lives in Epic 6.
- Faction validation (only allow valid faction names) is Epic 5's job — Character just stores the string.
- Blocks: Epics 5, 6, 7.
close_reason: Epic 4 (Character Typeclass & Persistent Stats) complete: C1 typeclass shipped (PR #12), C2 settings wired (PR #15), C3 unit tests (PR #17, 9 passing)

title: [T10.3] AGENTS.md polish — GridWars-specific guidance
description: Trim AGENTS.md (currently mostly bd/Blackrim boilerplate) and add GridWars-specific guidance: (1) project layout — gridwars/ game dir, vendor/evennia/ submodule, world/build_grid.py for sectors, commands/* for in-game cmds; (2) running the local server flow; (3) Evennia idioms that matter (AttributeProperty NOT .attributes.get(); typeclass paths relative to gridwars/; build_grid via Django shell not batchcode); (4) the no-character-deletion rule (defeat resets, never deletes). Keep the Blackrim/bd sections; ADD the gridwars-specific stuff. Acceptance: a new contributor agent can read AGENTS.md and start landing wave-style work without re-learning the lessons we already paid for.

title: [W3-followup] Fix world.build_grid docstring + SETUP.md: 'evennia batchcode' is in-game, not CLI
description: W3-redo agent discovered that build_grid.py docstring instructs `cd gridwars && evennia batchcode world.build_grid`, but `batchcode` is an in-game command (run from an authenticated session), NOT a CLI subcommand. The agent ran it via Django shell instead (`python -c "from world import build_grid; build_grid.build()"`). SETUP.md "Building the grid" section likely has the same incorrect invocation. Fix: update both the docstring and SETUP.md to show the actual working invocation. Acceptance: a fresh contributor can copy-paste the command and have it work without a running server session.
close_reason: shipped via PR #27

title: [I3] 'help gridwars' curated command index
description: Add a top-level help entry `help gridwars` that prints a curated command index for the playable slice: status, scan, faction, faction choose, strike, plus stock Evennia commands worth surfacing (look, say, pose, who). Use Evennias HelpEntry mechanism (gridwars/world/help_entries.py exists from `evennia --init` — extend it). Brief one-line description per command, organized by category (Info / Combat / Faction / Movement / Social). Acceptance: `help gridwars` returns the curated index; each listed command has its own working `help <cmd>`; renders cleanly in telnet + webclient.
close_reason: shipped via PR #25 (cbc79d5)

title: [S6] Document SPDX header convention for new source files
description: Document the SPDX header convention for new GridWars source files. Create `docs/CONVENTIONS.md` with: "New GridWars source files MUST begin with `# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later` (Python) or the language-appropriate comment equivalent. Vendored files inside `vendor/evennia/` retain their original SPDX/headers and MUST NOT be modified." Include one Python example and one YAML example. Cross-link to THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md. Acceptance: file exists; both examples present; CONTRIBUTING.md (when written in Epic 10) will link here.
close_reason: shipped via PR #4

title: [EPIC] Open-Source Polish (README, Contributing, Notices)
description:
# Epic 10 — Open-Source Polish (README, Contributing, Notices)

## Why
GridWars.run is intended to be open source from day one. Contributors and players arriving from GitHub will judge the project on the README. AGPL compliance also demands clean attribution and a contributor pathway. The README isn't just docs — it's the project's storefront.

## Scope
- Top-level `README.md`:
  - Hero line: "GridWars.run — Full PvP. No limits. Free to play. Open source."
  - One-paragraph pitch (cyber-grid, terminal-native, factions, programs, daemons).
  - "Get on the Grid" — local setup: clone w/ submodules, `make install`, `make migrate`, `make run`, connect via telnet/webclient.
  - "What's vendored": brief explanation of `vendor/evennia` and why.
  - License: AGPL-3.0-or-later for GridWars; BSD-3-Clause inside `vendor/evennia` (link THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md).
  - "First playable slice": list of in-game commands (`status`, `scan`, `faction`, `strike`).
  - "Roadmap": brief next-milestone tease (lightcycle duels, daemon AI, sector ownership) — non-binding.
  - Screenshot/asciinema placeholder (TODO with link to follow-up issue).
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`:
  - How to file an issue (link bd or GitHub Issues).
  - PR workflow (branch from main, run `make test`, sign off as required by AGPL/DCO if chosen).
  - Code style (PEP 8, `ruff` if added).
  - "Don't edit vendor/evennia" rule.
- `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` (created in Epic 1, refined here for tone).
- `AGENTS.md`: trim or augment to mention GridWars-specific agent guidance (vs. just bd boilerplate).

## Acceptance
- README renders cleanly on GitHub.
- All commands in setup section work on a fresh clone (verified by Tester or operator).
- CONTRIBUTING.md links to the test command + the "don't touch vendor/" rule.
- License + attribution links resolve.

## Model recommendation
- **Writer (Claude sonnet)** — README voice + pitch.
- **Writer (Claude haiku)** — CONTRIBUTING.md mechanical sections.
- **Reviewer (Claude sonnet)** — final pass for tone, accuracy, broken links.

## Notes
- Depends on Epics 1, 2, and at least one of the gameplay epics shipping so the "first playable slice" section is truthful.
- Can author drafts in parallel; verify-and-ship after gameplay lands.

value: [failure] Wave-1 S3+S5 builder (af6c37028ce051934) died with 'Output blocked by content filtering policy' after 9 tool uses. Likely trigger: verbatim AGPL preamble + full canonical text in brief. Retry strategy: keep AGPL text as a single curl+write, omit the in-brief preamble paragraph, and let agent compose a 2-line SPDX header inline instead of pasting the GNU warranty disclaimer into the brief.

[convention] /handoff exemption wired 2026-05-24: ~/.claude/hooks/delegation-discipline.sh skips Edit/Write counter for *handoff*.md under /tmp/, /private/tmp/, /var/folders/tc/k8ny6z4s14x73wys183cgkzw0000gn/T/. Backup at delegation-discipline.sh.bak.20260524. Smoke-tested: handoff path → no count change; regular path → still counted. Convention doc in blackrim CLAUDE.md 'Hand-off documents via /handoff' subsection.

[fix-and-convention] worktree-guard trapped-Gestalt bug patched 2026-05-24. The trapped-Gestalt branch of record-gestalt-session.sh (source ∈ resume|compact|clear AND cwd under worktrees/agent-*) now records session_id alongside the additionalContext warning. Without recording, the worktree-guard would treat Gestalt as if it were the agent owning that worktree and block escape cd. Canonical at /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/hooks-staging/record-gestalt-session.sh; deployed at /Users/jayse/.claude/hooks/record-gestalt-session.sh. Smoke-tested. BLACKRIM_DISABLE_WORKTREE_GUARD=1 env-var workaround REMOVED from ~/.claude/settings.json since no longer needed.

[Tester] Evennia GAME_DIR is resolved from os.getcwd() at settings-import time. Running evennia from the repo root always fails when the game directory is a subdirectory. Fix: cd into the game dir before any evennia invocation. evennia migrate also has an interactive superuser loop that hangs in non-TTY; use python -m django migrate instead, then make createsuperuser separately.

[convention] When dispatching ANY background agent via the Agent tool, invoke the /handoff skill first to write a structured hand-off doc to /tmp/ — it is the live-session global memory between agent boundaries. Reference its path in the spawn brief. Skill at /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/skills/handoff/SKILL.md; convention formalized in blackrim CLAUDE.md (Briefing agents before spawning → Hand-off documents subsection).
